name: Is solid hardwood or engineered hardwood better for our Bay Area climate?
headline: Is solid hardwood or engineered hardwood better for our Bay Area climate?

mainEntity.name: Is solid hardwood or engineered hardwood better for our Bay Area climate?
mainEntity.text: Is solid hardwood or engineered hardwood better for our Bay Area climate?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both solid hardwood and engineered hardwood can work in the Bay Area, but engineered is often the safer choice for many homes. Solid hardwood is a single piece of wood. It can be sanded and refinished several times, which is great for very long-term use. However, it is more sensitive to changes in moisture and temperature. In coastal climates or over concrete slabs and crawlspaces, solid wood is more likely to cup, gap, or swell if conditions are not controlled. Engineered hardwood has a real wood top layer glued over layers of plywood or similar material. This layered structure makes it more stable when humidity changes. It is usually better for lower levels, over concrete, and in homes where indoor humidity can vary. Many engineered floors can still be refinished once or twice, depending on the thickness of the top layer. If your home has good climate control and a wood subfloor, solid hardwood can be an excellent choice. If you are installing over a slab, near the coast, or you want a little more protection from movement, engineered hardwood is often recommended. A common mistake is choosing only by price or species without checking whether the product is approved for your type of subfloor and local conditions. Always follow the manufacturer’s rules for acclimation, installation, and moisture.

description: Both solid hardwood and engineered hardwood can work in the Bay Area, but engineered is often the safer choice for many homes. Solid hardwood is a single piece
